phase_name: session 22 complete — paper reproduced, proof path outlined

completed_tasks.name: W_p formula bug: q kernel from Lemma 2.3, not cos(2pi(n-m)lk/L)
Paper reproduced: all-positive QW, xi_hat zeros match zeta zeros
Zero accuracy plateau: 18 digits at N=30 regardless of lambda
mpmath.quad for WR diagonal (60+ digit accuracy in 0.1s)
Grok proof checks 1-3 run, check 4 Xi crash fixed

remaining_tasks.name: Fix normalization: F(0)=0 but Xi(0)=0.497 — structural sin(0)=0 issue
Rerun check 4 (Jensen integral) with fixed Xi_func
Investigate winding number: got -10 vs expected 20
Run N=60 at 100dp to confirm alpha~0.5 digits/mode
Share check results with Grok for normalization guidance
Write up proof sketch as LaTeX if checks pass

blockers.blocker: F_{lambda,N}(0) = 0 by construction (sin(0)=0 in secular eq) but Xi(0) = 0.497. Functions don't converge pointwise — lambda^{-iz} phase rotates everything. Grok's contour argument needs normalization adjustment.

decisions.decision: W_p uses q(U_n,U_m)(log k) from Lemma 2.3
mpmath.quad (tanh-sinh) for WR diagonal instead of midpoint rule
decisions.rationale: 0.1s for 60 digits vs 6.6s for 13 digits. Eliminates OFFSET entirely.

decisions.decision: Two-scale convergence law: lambda controls eps_N, N controls zero accuracy
Grok's proof path: almost-kernel + determinant convergence + Hurwitz, no prolate needed
decisions.rationale: Eigenvector freezing makes N->inf trivial. eps_N exponential decay handles lambda->inf. Contour integral bridges to uniform bound.

next_action: SESSION 23: (1) Fix the normalization issue — F and Xi differ structurally, need to understand how Grok's contour argument handles this (probably compare zeros, not functions). (2) Rerun all 4 checks with fixed Xi_func. (3) Share results with Grok for guidance on the normalization. (4) Run N=60 at 100dp to measure alpha precisely.
context_notes: Scripts: connes_attack.py (QW builder + Rayleigh), connes_proof_checks.py (4 Grok checks, Xi_func fixed), connes_session22.py (gamma_L). Key: W_p uses q kernel, mpmath.quad for diagonal. Sliwinski paper: arXiv:2601.12133 (lower bound 1/(4 ln lambda)). Python: /c/Python313/python.exe.
